notes: Author and subject of the memoir; reflects on fifty years in computing.
Kastner's research firm; acquired by Harte-Hanks. Described as shifting character dramatically post-acquisition.
Acquirer of Aberdeen Group; Kastner notes the acquisition 'created wealth and destroyed culture, more or less simultaneously.'
Kastner uses Stratus as the example of a niche defended not by patents but by the difficulty of making 'uptime' viscerally real to a buying committee.
Referenced as one of Kastner's major employers and as an example of the proprietary minicomputer transition.
Referenced in the context of the IBM System/360 Model 65 and the mainframe era.
Named as one of the institutions where Kastner encountered the recurring pattern of organizational resistance to technology.
